---
author: "Copyright: Optimization Team 2026"
date: "Report compiled on `r format(Sys.time(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output: html_document
title: "SFP Comparison Report on `r params$share` (`r params$ministry`)"
subtitle: "`r params$subline`"

params:
  old_data:
    label: "Input file 1:"
    value: "*.csv"
    input: text
  new_data:
    label: "Input file 2:"
    value: "*.csv"
    input: text
  ministry:
    label: "Ministry Name:"
    value: AF
    input: select
    choices: [AF, BCWS, EMLI, ECS, ENV, FOR, IRR, MCM, WLRS]
  share:
    label: "Share Name to Search:"
    value: "S01234"
    input: text
  subline:
    label: "Month YYYY and Month YYYY"
    value: "Sept 2025 and Mar 2026"
    input: text
  output_dir:
    value: "output"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.width = 7,
  fig.height = 5,
  fig.align = "center"
)

library(data.table)
library(DT)
library(dplyr)
library(forcats)
library(glue)
library(here)
library(knitr)
library(lubridate)
library(magrittr)
library(openxlsx)
library(purrr)
library(scales)
library(stringr)
library(stringi)
library(tidyverse)
library(zoo)
library(kableExtra)

# set working directory
here::here()
```

```{r global, call-external-functions, include = FALSE}
# load custom functions from external scripts
source(here("scripts", glue('custom_sfp_functions.R')), local = knitr::knit_global())
```

```{r load data, include = FALSE}
# load csv and fill empty cells 
sfp_old <- read_csv(params$old_data, na = c("", " ", "NULL"))
sfp_new <- read_csv(params$new_data, na = c(""," ","NULL"))

# keep only 7 relevant columns
sfp_old <- sfp_old[,c(1:2,5:9)]
sfp_new <- sfp_new[,c(1:2,5:9)]

# rename columns

colnames(sfp_old) <- c("filename", "path", "sizemb","lastaccessdate","modificationdate","creationdate","share")
colnames(sfp_new) <- colnames(sfp_old)

head(sfp_old)
head(sfp_new)
```

```{r format and filter, include = FALSE}
format_sfp_data <- function(x,y) {
  x %>%
  mutate(
    share = gsub("\\", "", share, fixed = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(str_detect(share, {y})) %>%
  mutate(
    folder_path = stringr::word(path, 1, 8, sep = "\\\\"),
    sizegb = round(sizemb / 1000, 3),
    cost = round(sizemb / 1000, 3) * 2.7,
    lastaccessdate   = convert.date(lastaccessdate),
    modificationdate = convert.date(modificationdate),
    creationdate     = convert.date(creationdate),
    # <<< one-line remap of path values >>>
    path = remap_substrings(path),
    folder_path = remap_substrings(folder_path)) %>% 
  select(filename, folder_path, path, sizemb, sizegb, cost, lastaccessdate, modificationdate, creationdate)
}

sfp_old_data <- format_sfp_data(sfp_old, params$share)
sfp_new_data <- format_sfp_data(sfp_new, params$share)

head(sfp_old_data)
head(sfp_new_data)
```

```{r find duplicate files, include = FALSE}
# find files that are duplicated in both name and size
find_duplicates <- function(x) {
  duplicate_files <- x %>% 
  group_by(filename, sizegb, folder_path) %>% 
  filter( n() > 1 )
}

sfp_old_dupes_find <- find_duplicates(sfp_old_data)
sfp_new_dupes_find <- find_duplicates(sfp_new_data)

files_duplicated <- function(x) {
  x[!duplicated(x[ , c("filename", "sizegb", "folder_path")]), ] %>% 
  mutate(duplicate_count = n()) %>%
  group_by(folder_path) %>% 
  summarize(duplicates = sum(duplicate_count)) %>% 
  select(folder_path, duplicates)
}

dupe_files_old <- files_duplicated(sfp_old_dupes_find)
dupe_files_new <- files_duplicated(sfp_new_dupes_find)

join_dupes <- full_join(dupe_files_old, dupe_files_new, by = "folder_path") %>%
  rename(
    "folder path" = folder_path,
    "old dupe count" = duplicates.x,
    "new dupe count" = duplicates.y
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    across(c("old dupe count", "new dupe count"), ~replace_na(., 0))
  )

join_dupes$"dupe count change" <- as.numeric(join_dupes$"old dupe count") - as.numeric(join_dupes$"new dupe count")

head(join_dupes)
```


```{r calculate folder totals, include = FALSE}
calc_folder_totals <- function(x) {
  x %>% 
    group_by(folder_path) %>% 
    summarize("folder size gb" = sum(sizegb), "number of files" = n(), "folder cost" = sum(cost)) %>% 
    select("folder path" = folder_path, "folder size gb", "number of files", "folder cost")
}

old_totals <- calc_folder_totals(sfp_old_data)
new_totals<- calc_folder_totals(sfp_new_data)

head(old_totals)
head(new_totals)
```

```{r join old and new tables, include = FALSE}

join_totals <- full_join(old_totals, new_totals, by = "folder path") %>%
  select(
    "folder path",
    "old folder size gb" = "folder size gb.x",
    "new folder size gb" = "folder size gb.y",
    "old file count" = "number of files.x",
    "new file count" = "number of files.y",
    "old folder cost" = "folder cost.x",
    "new folder cost" = "folder cost.y"
  ) %>%

  # Replace NA in numeric fields with 0
  mutate(
    across(
      c(
        "old folder size gb", "new folder size gb",
        "old file count", "new file count",
        "old folder cost", "new folder cost"
      ),
      ~replace_na(., 0)
    )
  )


join_totals <- join_totals %>%
  mutate(
    "folder size change gb" = `new folder size gb` - `old folder size gb`,
    "file count change" = `new file count` - `old file count`,
    "folder cost change" = `new folder cost` - `old folder cost`
  )


join_totals <- full_join(join_totals, join_dupes, by = "folder path") %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      c("old dupe count", "new dupe count"),
      ~replace_na(., 0)
    ),
    "dupe count change" = `new dupe count` - `old dupe count`
  )

epsilon <- 1e-6

join_totals <- join_totals %>%
  mutate(
    status = case_when(
      # New folder (only appears in new dataset)
      `old folder size gb` == 0 & `new folder size gb` > 0 ~ "New",

      # Removed folder (only appears in old dataset)
      `old folder size gb` > 0 & `new folder size gb` == 0 ~ "Removed",

      # No change across key metrics
      abs(`folder size change gb`) < epsilon &
      abs(`file count change`) == 0 &
      abs(`folder cost change`) < epsilon &
      abs(`dupe count change`) == 0 ~ "Unchanged",

      TRUE ~ "Modified"
    )
  )

join_totals <- join_totals %>%
  select(status,	"folder path", "old folder size gb", "new folder size gb", "folder size change gb", "old folder cost", "new folder cost", "folder cost change", "old file count", "new file count", "file count change", "old dupe count", "new dupe count", "dupe count change")

head(join_totals)
```

```{r dynamic output name, include = FALSE}
# create output file name based on parameters
output_excel <- paste0(
  params$ministry, "_",
  "SFP_Report_Comparison_",
  params$share, "_",
  params$subline,
  ".xlsx"
)
```

```{r write output to Excel file, include = FALSE}
# create workbook
excel <- createWorkbook()

# Create worksheet
addWorksheet(excel, "Folder Comparison Details")

# Write data to worksheet
writeData(excel, "Folder Comparison Details", join_totals)

# (Optional) Add filter
addFilter(excel, "Folder Comparison Details", row = 1, cols = 1:2)

# freeze top row of all sheets
freezePane(excel, sheet = 1, firstRow = TRUE)

# set conditional colour format on columns 4, 7, and 10 for values < 0
sty1 <- createStyle(fontColour = "#000080")

conditionalFormatting(
  excel,
  sheet = 1,
  cols = c(5, 8, 11, 14, 17),
  rows = 2:(nrow(join_totals) + 1),
  rule = "<0",
  style = sty1
)

# Example: highlight "New" rows (green)
conditionalFormatting(
  excel,
  sheet = 1,
  cols = 1:ncol(join_totals),
  rows = 2:(nrow(join_totals) + 1),
  rule = '=$A2="New"',
  style = createStyle(bgFill = "#C6EFCE")
)

# Highlight "Removed" (red)
conditionalFormatting(
  excel,
  sheet = 1,
  cols = 1:ncol(join_totals),
  rows = 2:(nrow(join_totals) + 1),
  rule = '=$A2="Removed"',
  style = createStyle(bgFill = "#FFC7CE")
)

# set currency format on column
sty2 = createStyle(numFmt="$0.00")
addStyle(excel, sheet = 1, sty2, rows=2:(nrow(join_totals)+1), cols=6)
addStyle(excel, sheet = 1, sty2, rows=2:(nrow(join_totals)+1), cols=7)
addStyle(excel, sheet = 1, sty2, rows=2:(nrow(join_totals)+1), cols=8)

# set custom column widths for all sheets
setColWidths(excel, sheet = 1, cols = c(1, 2, 3:17), widths = c(25, 75, 25, 25, 25, 25, 25, 25, 25, 25, 25, 25, 25, 25))

# save the workbook to file
if (!dir.exists(params$output_dir)) {
  dir.create(params$output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

if (!exists("join_totals") || nrow(join_totals) == 0) {
  stop("join_totals is empty — nothing to write to Excel")
}

saveWorkbook(
  excel,
  file = file.path(params$output_dir, output_excel),
  overwrite = TRUE
)
```
